content.problemStatement:
Jonas asked Martha to stand at point (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) on an infinite cartesian plane. He gave her a string SSS which consists of operations in the form of characters **'R'**, **'L'**, **'U'**, and
**'D'**.

Each character of the string corresponds to the following movements:


**'R'** means moving right by 111 unit i.e, (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) to (x+1,y)(x+1,y)(x+1,y).
**'L'** means moving left by 111 unit i.e, (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) to (x‚àí1,y)(x-1,y)(x‚àí1,y).
**'U'** means moving up by 111 unit i.e, (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) to (x,y+1)(x,y+1)(x,y+1).
**'D'** means moving down by 111 unit i.e, (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) to (x,y‚àí1)(x,y-1)(x,y‚àí1).
Now, Jonas asks Martha QQQ queries. In each query, coordinates are given of form (x2,y2)(x_2,y_2)(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã). Martha needs to tell if it is possible to move from (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) to (x2,y2)(x_2,y_2)(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã) by choosing a **subsequence** of given string SSS and performing the operations in sequential order.Starting point (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) remains same for all queries.

If it is possible, print **"YES"** and the length of the smallest possible subsequence of string, else print **"NO"**.

Recall that string aaa is a **subsequence** of a string bbb if aaa can be obtained from bbb by deletion of several (possibly zero or all) characters. For example, for the string aaa="codechef", the following strings are subsequences: "code", "ee", "odchef", "df", "", and others, but the following are not subsequences: "ced", "eehc", "cofe".

**Note**: Use of Fast IO is recommended.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then test cases follow.
For each test case, first line will contain string SSS, string of operations.
Second line will contain coordinates x1x_1x1‚Äã and y1y_1y1‚Äã.
Third line will contain QQQ, number of queries.
QQQ lines follow. Each line will contain coordinates x2x_2x2‚Äã and y2y_2y2‚Äã.
###Output:
For each query if subsequence exists then print "YES" (without quotes) and length of smallest possible subsequence separated by a space in new line, else print "NO" (without quotes) in new line.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§101 \leq T \leq 101‚â§T‚â§10
1‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§1061 \leq |S| \leq 10^61‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§106
‚àí108‚â§x1,x2,y1,y2‚â§108-10^8 \leq x_1,x_2,y_1,y_2 \leq 10^8‚àí108‚â§x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,y1‚Äã,y2‚Äã‚â§108
1‚â§Q‚â§1051 \leq Q \leq 10^51‚â§Q‚â§105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Given String is "RLUDRLUD", (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) is (1,2)(1,2)(1,2). We can move from (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) to (2,3)(2,3)(2,3) in just two operations if we choose subsequence as "RU" or "UR".
Suppose we choose "RU" as a subsequence. 'R' means moving from (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) to (2,2)(2,2)(2,2). 'U' means moving from (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) to (2,3)(2,3)(2,3).
In the second query, we can't move from (1,2) to (1,-2) using operations of the given string.
